info.title: Universal Document AI API
info.description: AI-powered document editing with multi-tenant organization support

post.summary: Edit, draft, or restructure a document using natural language. Preserves tables, styling, and formatting.
post.description: Synchronous AI chat that can rewrite specific paragraphs, add or remove table rows, restructure sections, generate new content from templates, or transform an entire document. Pass document_html to give the AI context. Returns AI response text plus structural document changes (HTML edits, additions, deletions) with chunk IDs. One billable operation per document-modifying turn. For long-running edits or human-in-the-loop approval, use chat_async. Optional: model_tier (core/turbo/pro/max), thinking_depth (fast/balanced/deep), image_attachments for multimodal vision. RECOMMENDED for AI agents working with large documents (>20 pages): set response_mode='compact' to skip the full HTML in the response (the AI returns only per-section diffs in chunk_diffs) and save thousands of tokens per turn. To read sections in compact mode, just send a natural-language request like 'show me the force majeure section' — the AI returns the content in the reply text. Always use natural language to describe what you want; the AI handles all internal section lookups.

get.summary: List your active document editing sessions to resume or audit prior work.
get.description: Returns sessions sorted by most recent activity, with message counts and last-updated timestamps. Each session represents one document with full edit history and AI conversation persisted server-side. Use to find a previous editing context to resume (then call get_session_history) or to audit your workspace.

parameters.name: user_id

schema.description: Filter by user ID (deprecated - auth determines scope)
schema.title: User Id

parameters.description: Filter by user ID (deprecated - auth determines scope)

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of sessions to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of sessions to return

get.summary: Restore the full conversation and document state for a previous session.
get.description: Returns complete message history (user and AI), final document HTML with chunk IDs preserved, attachment list, and editor actions (font, color, alignment changes). Use to continue editing a document you started in a prior session. The AI rehydrates with full context of all prior decisions, chunk IDs, and attachments.

post.description:
Upload a document attachment to a session for AI reference.

The file is processed asynchronously — text is extracted, converted, and indexed
so the AI can search and reference it during chat. Use GET /v1/attachments/status/{session_id}
to check processing progress.

Supported file types: .pdf, .docx, .txt, .rtf, .md, .html, .htm (max 50 MB).
Returns a job_id for tracking the processing status.

delete.summary: Remove an attachment from a session or cancel its in-progress processing.
delete.description: Removes the attachment from the session; the AI will no longer reference it in subsequent chat turns. If processing is still in progress, also cancels the underlying job. Use to free up context, remove sensitive files mid-session, or replace a stale reference document. The attachment_id can be either the final attachment ID or the job ID (during processing).

get.summary: Check processing status of all attachments in a session.
get.description: Returns each attachment's processing state (pending/processing/completed/failed) plus extracted text length and chunk count once ready. Poll this after upload_attachment_base64 to know when an attachment becomes queryable by the AI. Surfaces processing errors with actionable messages.

post.description:
Upload a document file and load it into the editor.

Converts the file to HTML with formatting preserved (tables, colors, images).
Images are extracted to cloud storage and referenced by URL.
Returns the HTML for the frontend editor to display.

AI indexing happens automatically on the first chat message.
For API clients who want immediate indexing, pass ?index=true.

parameters.name: index

schema.description: Set to 'true' for immediate AI indexing (API clients). Default: conversion only.
schema.title: Index

parameters.description: Set to 'true' for immediate AI indexing (API clients). Default: conversion only.

post.summary: Export the current document as a styled .doc, .docx, .pdf, or .html file with full fidelity.
post.description: Round-trips the document through the original docx renderer, preserving tables, borders, shading, alternating row colors, headers, footers, fonts, inline styling, and embedded images. Two modes: pass html directly to export ad-hoc content, OR pass session_id to export the session's current state. Format options: 'doc' (default, Word-compatible HTML, pixel-perfect fidelity, best for end users) or 'docx' (native Open XML via python-docx, best for programmatic processing or mail merge). Fidelity is the differentiator vs naive HTML to docx converters.

post.summary: Upload .docx/PDF/HTML/MD/RTF as the active editable document with chunk-ID structural editing.
post.description: Parses the file into structured HTML where every paragraph, heading, table, row, and cell has a unique chunk ID, enabling the AI to make targeted structural edits via chat ("remove row 3 of the pricing table" works). Tables, borders, shading, alternating row colors, fonts, and inline styling are preserved on edit and export. Also works for AI-generated content: if you've drafted an outline or partial document in your context, upload it here as the working doc, then use chat to fill in the rest. For files >100KB, prefer request_upload_url (pre-signed URL flow, ships in Phase B) to avoid token bloat from base64 through the agent context. Supported formats: .pdf, .docx, .txt, .rtf, .md, .html, .htm. Max 50 MB.

post.summary: Upload a reference file (PDF/DOCX/image) for the AI to query while editing.
post.description: Files are processed asynchronously and become AI-searchable once ready. The AI can then reference the attachment's content during chat (e.g., "rewrite section 3 to match the style guide PDF I attached"). Images are queryable via multimodal vision. Poll get_attachment_status to know when ready. Distinct from upload_document_base64: attachments are read-only context for the AI to reference, not the editable working document. Supported formats: .pdf, .docx, .txt, .rtf, .md, .html, .htm. Max 50 MB. Requires session_id.

post.summary: Save a document template (NDA, contract, SOP, letterhead) for reuse across sessions.
post.description: Templates persist across sessions and can be referenced by the AI when drafting new documents (e.g., "draft an NDA using my standard template"). Stored at user or organization scope. Ideal for boilerplate, branded letterheads, recurring document structures, or compliance-required templates. Supports the same formats as document upload: .docx, .pdf, .txt, .rtf, .md. Max 50 MB.

get.summary: List your async chat jobs (in-progress, awaiting approval, completed, failed).
get.description: Returns jobs sorted by most recent, optionally filtered by status. Use to monitor long-running AI edits started via chat_async, see which jobs are paused waiting for human approval, or audit completed work. Each job tracks the chat that started it, the changes made, and any HITL decisions logged.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of jobs to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of jobs to return

get.summary: Get the status, partial results, and any pending changes for an async chat job.
get.description: Returns the job's current state (pending, in_progress, awaiting_approval, completed, failed, or cancelled), intermediate AI responses streamed during execution, the final document HTML once complete, and any pending changes awaiting user approval (with chunk IDs and proposed HTML diffs in metadata.pending_changes). Poll this after chat_async to track progress and retrieve results. When status is awaiting_approval, call POST /v1/chat/{session_id}/approve to approve or deny each pending change.

post.summary: Cancel a pending or in-progress async chat job.
post.description: Stops the AI mid-edit. Already-applied changes are preserved in the document; pending changes are discarded. Use to abort long-running operations that are no longer needed (e.g., user changed their mind, or you want to retry with different parameters or model_tier). Only jobs with status pending or processing can be cancelled. Returns the updated job details with status cancelled.

get.summary: List all async chat jobs for a specific session, most recent first.
get.description: Returns the full job history of one document. Useful for auditing what the AI did to a document over time, or finding a specific job that's waiting for HITL approval. Same shape as list_jobs but scoped to one session.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of jobs to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of jobs to return

post.summary: Start a long-running or HITL-approved AI edit; returns a job_id to poll for results.
post.description: Use instead of chat when (a) the edit is large or multi-step, (b) you need human approval on each proposed change before it applies (set approval_mode='ask_every_time'), or (c) you can't afford to block on a synchronous response. Returns a job_id immediately. Poll get_job to track progress and retrieve the final document. Approve or deny pending changes via approve_change. Job state is durable and survives server restarts. Optional: model_tier (core/turbo/pro/max), thinking_depth (fast/balanced/deep), image_attachments for multimodal vision. RECOMMENDED for AI agents working with large documents (>20 pages): set response_mode='compact' so the job result skips the full HTML and surfaces only per-section diffs in chunk_diffs, saving thousands of tokens per poll. To read sections in compact mode, just send a natural-language chat request ('show me the pricing section') — the AI returns the content in the reply text. Always use natural language; the AI handles all internal section lookups. HITL workflow (approval_mode='ask_every_time'): 1) poll get_job until status=awaiting_approval, 2) read metadata.pending_changes for proposed edits, 3) call approve_change per change, 4) continue polling until status=completed.

get.description:
Stream real-time progress for a chat job using Server-Sent Events (SSE).

Opens an SSE connection that streams events as the AI processes your request.
Use this with the job_id returned from POST /v1/chat/async.

Event types:
- 'intermediate': Progress updates during processing (content, sequence, timestamp).
- 'proposed_change': A document change proposed for review (when approval_mode is 'ask_every_time').
- 'final': Processing complete. Contains the full result with AI response and document changes.
- 'usage': Billing data emitted after 'final' (monthly_used, monthly_limit, monthly_remaining).
- 'error': An error occurred (job failed, cancelled, or not found).

Authentication: Pass a token or api_key as a query parameter (required for EventSource which cannot set headers).

parameters.name: job_id

schema.description: Job ID to stream progress for
schema.title: Job Id

parameters.description: Job ID to stream progress for

parameters.name: token

schema.description: Authentication token (query parameter for EventSource compatibility)
schema.title: Token

parameters.description: Authentication token (query parameter for EventSource compatibility)

parameters.name: api_key

schema.description: API key (query parameter for EventSource compatibility)
schema.title: Api Key

parameters.description: API key (query parameter for EventSource compatibility)

post.summary: Approve or deny AI-proposed document changes one-by-one or in batch (HITL workflow).
post.description: Used with chat_async when approval_mode='ask_every_time'. For each proposed change (with chunk_id and HTML diff), respond approved=true|false plus optional feedback for the AI to revise on. Approved changes apply atomically; denied changes are discarded; the AI may revise based on feedback in the next turn. Required for regulated workflows (legal, medical, compliance) where every AI edit must be reviewed before it touches the document. For single changes: set approved=true/false and optionally provide feedback. For batch decisions: provide a 'changes' array with per-change decisions. After approval, the job resumes processing and eventually reaches status=completed.

get.description:
Long-poll for real-time job updates on a session (B2B organizations only).

Returns immediately if there are recent job updates, or holds the connection
open up to the specified timeout. Use the 'since' parameter to avoid receiving
duplicate updates.

parameters.name: since

schema.description: ISO 8601 timestamp — only return updates after this time
schema.title: Since

parameters.description: ISO 8601 timestamp — only return updates after this time

parameters.name: timeout

schema.description: Long polling timeout in seconds (max 1800 = 30 minutes)
schema.title: Timeout

parameters.description: Long polling timeout in seconds (max 1800 = 30 minutes)

post.description:
Upload a document file as a personal/organization template.

The file is processed synchronously: text extraction, HTML conversion, and content indexing.
Supported formats: .docx, .pdf, .txt, .rtf, .md
Templates are scoped to the uploading user or organization.

get.summary: List all saved document templates available to the user or organization.
get.description: Returns active (non-deleted) templates with name, format, size, and creation date metadata. Use to show the AI what reusable document structures are available for drafting new documents. Templates are scoped to the authenticated entity: users via Firebase or sk_ key see only their own templates; organizations via lce_ key see theirs.

delete.summary: Delete a saved document template by ID.
delete.description: Soft-deletes the template; only the owner (user or organization) can delete. Once deleted, the template no longer appears in list_user_templates and cannot be referenced by the AI for new documents. Existing documents already drafted from the template are unaffected.

get.description:
Get current user's profile information

Returns user profile with subscription tier and usage limits

patch.description:
Update current user's profile

Allows updating display name, timezone, language, and preferences

get.description:
Get detailed usage statistics for current user

Returns operation counts, token usage, and success rates by operation type

List all chat sessions for current user

Returns list of sessions with last activity and message counts

schema.title: Response Get User Sessions V1 Users Me Sessions Get

delete.description:
Delete a specific chat session

Removes all messages for the given session ID (user must own the session)

get.description:
Check current usage limits and remaining operations

Returns real-time usage information with reset date

List all API keys for the current user (masked).

Returns key prefix and last 4 characters for identification.

schema.title: Response List Api Keys V1 Users Me Api Keys Get

post.description:
Create a new API key for the current user.

The raw key is returned ONCE in the response. It cannot be retrieved again.

delete.description:
Revoke (soft delete) an API key.

The key will be marked as inactive and can no longer be used for authentication.

post.description:
Create a checkout session to upgrade your subscription.

Returns a URL to redirect the user to the hosted payment page.
Only allows upgrading to a higher tier than the current plan.

get.description:
Get a URL to the customer billing portal.

The portal allows managing your subscription, viewing invoices,
and updating your payment method.

post.description:
Cancel the current subscription at end of billing period.

The user retains full access to their current tier until the billing
period ends. After that, Polar sends a subscription.revoked webhook
which downgrades the user to the free tier automatically.

Receive and process webhook events from Polar.
Uses Polar SDK signature verification — NOT Firebase auth.
Handles 8 events: 7 subscription lifecycle + order.created for billing cycle reset.

Redeem a promo code and add the granted operations to the authenticated user's account.

B2C only: Firebase tokens and sk_ user API keys are accepted; lce_ org keys are rejected.
Requires a verified email. Each user may redeem a given code at most once.

Rate-limited to 3 attempts per IP per hour. All attempts (success and failure) are
logged for audit.

get.description:
List the authenticated user's promotion redemptions, split into `active` (drawable now)
and `history` (exhausted / expired / revoked).

Used by the Settings → Billing tab to render the Credits & Promotions section.

post.summary: Get a pre-signed URL to upload large files (.docx/PDF/HTML/MD/RTF) without bloating agent context.
post.description: Returns a short-lived (5-minute) PUT URL plus a curl example. **If you have shell access (Bash tool), execute the returned `curl_example` yourself to push the file directly to cloud storage — do NOT paste the curl command back to the user expecting them to run it manually. Only fall back to returning the curl text when shell execution is genuinely unavailable.** Bytes never pass through the agent's context window either way. After upload completes, call process_uploaded_document with the upload_id to trigger parsing. For files <100KB where token cost is trivial, upload_document_base64 still works inline. Max file size: 100 MB. Supported formats: .pdf, .docx, .txt, .rtf, .md, .html, .htm.

post.summary: Parse an uploaded file into structured HTML with chunk IDs for targeted AI editing.
post.description: Fetches the file uploaded via request_upload_url and runs the same parsing pipeline as upload_document_base64. Returns structured HTML with unique chunk IDs on every paragraph, heading, table, row, and cell — enabling the AI to make targeted structural edits via chat ("remove row 3 of the pricing table" works). Tables, borders, shading, alternating row colors, fonts, and inline styling preserved on edit and export. Counts as one billable operation. Specify parse_mode='document' to load as the active editable document, or parse_mode='attachment' to load as read-only AI-searchable reference.

post.summary: Get a pre-signed URL to download an exported document without proxying through the agent.
post.description: Returns a short-lived (15-minute) GET URL plus a curl example. **If you have shell access (Bash tool), execute the returned `curl_example` yourself to download the file to the user's working directory — do NOT paste the curl command back to the user expecting them to run it manually. Only fall back to returning the URL+command as text when shell execution is genuinely unavailable, OR when the user has explicitly asked for the URL only.** Bytes never pass through the agent's context window either way. Internally this calls the same export pipeline as export_document, then stores the result in cloud storage and returns a signed URL. Specify format='pdf', 'docx', 'doc', or 'html'.

get.summary: Verify the SuperDocs MCP server is reachable and serving traffic.
get.description: Returns 200 with {"status":"healthy"} when the API is up. Call this once after MCP install to confirm the connection works before invoking other tools. No authentication required.

Deep health check that verifies the job queue, AI workflow state store, and database are all responsive within a bounded time budget. Used by container orchestrators as a liveness probe to distinguish "process alive and TCP listening" (what /health reports) from "application is actually serving requests" (this endpoint).

redemption_id.title: Redemption Id
redemption_id.description: Unique identifier for this promotion grant.

name.title: Name
name.description: Human-friendly promotion name (e.g., 'YC SUS India 2026 cohort').

ops_remaining.title: Ops Remaining
ops_remaining.description: Operations still available in this promotion bucket.

ops_granted.title: Ops Granted
ops_granted.description: Total operations originally granted by this redemption.

expires_at.title: Expires At
expires_at.description: ISO 8601 expiry timestamp. Null if the credits never expire.

ActivePromotionInfo.title: ActivePromotionInfo
ActivePromotionInfo.description: A single active (drawable) promotion belonging to the authenticated user.

ActivePromotionOut.title: ActivePromotionOut
ActivePromotionOut.description: A single active (drawable) promotion grant belonging to the current user.

last_four.title: Last Four
last_four.description: Last 4 characters of the key for identification.

is_active.title: Is Active
is_active.description: Whether the key is active. Revoked keys show as false.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the key was created.

last_used_at.title: Last Used At
last_used_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of last use, or null if never used.

job_id.title: Job Id
job_id.description: Job ID that is awaiting approval.

change_id.title: Change Id
change_id.description: Specific change to approve/deny (for individual decisions). Get change IDs from the job's pending_changes.

approved.title: Approved
approved.description: Whether to approve (true) or deny (false) the change.

feedback.title: Feedback
feedback.description: Optional feedback explaining why a change was denied. Helps the AI adjust future suggestions.

changes.title: Changes
changes.description: Batch decisions: array of {change_id, approved, feedback} objects. Use for 'Accept All' or 'Deny All'.

ApprovalRequest.title: ApprovalRequest
ApprovalRequest.description: Approve or deny proposed AI document changes.

message.title: Message
message.description: Your message to the AI assistant.

session_id.title: Session Id
session_id.description: Unique session identifier. Reuse to continue a conversation.

document_html.title: Document Html
document_html.description: Current document HTML. Include data-chunk-id attributes on elements to enable targeted AI edits.

user_id.title: User Id
user_id.description: User identifier. Automatically set from authentication — typically omit this.

async_mode.title: Async Mode
async_mode.description: Must be true for async processing. Included for backward compatibility.

model_tier.title: Model Tier
model_tier.description: AI model to use: 'core' (default, fast), 'turbo' (fastest), 'pro' (advanced reasoning), 'max' (most capable).

thinking_depth.title: Thinking Depth
thinking_depth.description: Reasoning depth: 'fast', 'balanced' (default), or 'deep'. Controls how much analysis the AI performs.

approval_mode.title: Approval Mode
approval_mode.description: Change review mode: 'approve_all' (default, auto-applies changes) or 'ask_every_time' (pauses for your review).

response_mode.title: Response Mode
response_mode.description: Response shape control. 'full' (default) puts the complete updated document HTML in the job result — required by web app editors and recommended for small documents. 'compact' (recommended for AI agents editing large documents) suppresses the full HTML and stores only per-section diffs (chunk_diffs), saving thousands of tokens when polling get_job. To read sections in compact mode, send a natural-language request to chat ('show me the pricing section') — the AI returns the content in the reply text.

AsyncChatRequest.title: AsyncChatRequest
AsyncChatRequest.description: Start an async chat request that processes in the background.

job_id.title: Job Id
job_id.description: Job identifier. Poll GET /v1/jobs/{job_id} for status and results.

AsyncChatResponse.title: AsyncChatResponse
AsyncChatResponse.description: Response from starting an async chat request.

filename.title: Filename
filename.description: Original filename with extension (e.g. 'contract.pdf'). Used for file type detection.

session_id.title: Session Id
session_id.description: Session ID. Auto-generated if not provided.

Base64UploadRequest.title: Base64UploadRequest
Base64UploadRequest.description: JSON request body for base64-encoded file uploads.

active_until.title: Active Until
active_until.description: ISO 8601 datetime until which the user retains access. Null if unknown.

document_changes.description: Document modifications made by the AI. Present only when the document was changed.

usage.description: Operation usage data. Present for authenticated users with usage tracking.

ChatResponse.title: ChatResponse
ChatResponse.description: AI response with optional document changes and usage data.

examples.response: I've added a confidentiality clause to section 3 covering non-disclosure obligations.

tier.title: Tier
tier.description: Target subscription tier: 'plus' ($20/mo, 2000 ops) or 'pro' ($99/mo, 10000 ops).

checkout_url.title: Checkout Url
checkout_url.description: URL to redirect the user to for payment. Opens the hosted checkout page.

name.title: Name
name.description: A label for this key (e.g., 'My App', 'CI Pipeline').

id.title: Id
id.description: Key identifier (UUID). Use this to revoke the key later.

name.title: Name
name.description: Label you assigned to this key.

key.title: Key
key.description: The full API key (sk_... format). Copy this now — it cannot be retrieved again.

key_prefix.title: Key Prefix
key_prefix.description: First 7 characters of the key (e.g., 'sk_a1b2') for identification.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the key was created.

CreateApiKeyResponse.title: CreateApiKeyResponse
CreateApiKeyResponse.description: Newly created API key. The raw key is shown only once — save it immediately.

updated_html.title: Updated Html
updated_html.description: Full updated document HTML with data-chunk-id attributes. Apply this to your editor to sync the document. Present in 'full' response mode (default); null in 'compact' mode.

version_id.title: Version Id
version_id.description: Document version identifier. Changes on every modification.

changes_summary.title: Changes Summary
changes_summary.description: Human-readable summary of what was changed.

requires_approval.title: Requires Approval
requires_approval.description: If true, changes need review via the approve endpoint before being applied.

pending_changes.title: Pending Changes
pending_changes.description: Proposed changes awaiting approval. Present when requires_approval is true.

changes.title: Changes
changes.description: History of individual changes applied during this request.

chunk_diffs.title: Chunk Diffs
chunk_diffs.description: Per-section before/after content for changes that were applied this turn. Present in 'compact' response mode (when updated_html is null) so the agent can verify what was modified without paying token cost for the full document. Same shape as pending_changes.

DocumentChanges.title: DocumentChanges
DocumentChanges.description: Document modifications produced by the AI assistant.

html_content.title: Html Content
html_content.description: Full document HTML content with data-chunk-id attributes.

chunk_count.title: Chunk Count
chunk_count.description: Number of content sections in the document.

last_modified.title: Last Modified
last_modified.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the last modification.

attachments.title: Attachments
attachments.description: Session attachments. Each item has: id (string), filename (string), file_extension (string), processing_status ('ready', 'processing', or 'failed').

DocumentState.title: DocumentState
DocumentState.description: Current document state for session restoration.

session_id.title: Session Id
session_id.description: Session ID whose current document should be exported and packaged for download.

format.title: Format
format.description: Export format. 'doc' = Word-compatible HTML (highest fidelity), 'docx' = native OOXML, 'pdf' = portable, 'html' = raw markup.

filename.title: Filename
filename.description: Optional Content-Disposition filename for the download (without extension). Auto-detected from the document's first heading if not provided.

messages.title: Messages
messages.description: Ordered list of messages. Each item has: id (string), sender ('user' or 'ai'), content (string), timestamp (ISO 8601), turn_index (int).

document_state.description: Current document state. Present if the session has an active document.

editor_action.title: Editor Action
editor_action.description: Frontend instruction: 'update' (load document_state HTML into editor), 'clear' (reset editor), or 'keep' (no change).

EnhancedChatHistoryResponse.title: EnhancedChatHistoryResponse
EnhancedChatHistoryResponse.description: Conversation history with document state and restoration instructions.

session_id.title: Session Id
session_id.description: Session ID to export document from (Mode B). Required if html is not provided.

html.title: Html
html.description: HTML content to convert to DOCX (Mode A). If provided, this is used instead of the session document.

filename.title: Filename
filename.description: Custom filename for the exported file (without extension). Auto-detected from first heading if not provided.

format.title: Format
format.description: Export format: 'doc' (Word-compatible HTML — pixel-perfect formatting fidelity) or 'docx' (native Open XML — better for programmatic processing). Default: 'doc'.

HistoricalPromotionOut.title: HistoricalPromotionOut
HistoricalPromotionOut.description: An exhausted, expired, or revoked promotion grant kept for the user's history view.

ImageAttachmentData.title: ImageAttachmentData
ImageAttachmentData.description: Inline image attachment for vision-based analysis.

file_size.title: File Size
file_size.description: File size in bytes (attachment processing jobs).

content_type.title: Content Type
content_type.description: MIME type of uploaded file (attachment processing jobs).

document_html_provided.title: Document Html Provided
document_html_provided.description: Whether document HTML was included in the request (chat jobs).

pending_changes.title: Pending Changes
pending_changes.description: Proposed changes awaiting approval. Present when job status is 'awaiting_approval'.

intermediate_responses.title: Intermediate Responses
intermediate_responses.description: Progress updates during processing. Each item has: type, content, sequence, timestamp.

JobMetadata.title: JobMetadata
JobMetadata.description: Job metadata. Contents vary by job type.

job_id.title: Job Id
job_id.description: Unique job identifier. Use this to poll for status updates.

organization_id.title: Organization Id
organization_id.description: Organization that owns this job (null for user jobs).

user_id.title: User Id
user_id.description: User that owns this job (null for organization jobs).

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the job was created.

updated_at.title: Updated At
updated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the last status change.

result.description: Job output. Present when status is 'completed'. Contains AI response and document changes for chat jobs.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error message. Present when status is 'failed'.

metadata.description: Job metadata and context. Contains pending_changes when status is 'awaiting_approval'.

JobResponse.title: JobResponse
JobResponse.description: Status and details of an async job.

JobResult.title: JobResult
JobResult.description: Job result data. Structure depends on job type.

change_id.title: Change Id
change_id.description: Unique identifier for this proposed change.

chunk_id.title: Chunk Id
chunk_id.description: Target document section ID being modified or deleted.

old_html.title: Old Html
old_html.description: Previous HTML content of the section (for updates).

new_html.title: New Html
new_html.description: Proposed new HTML content (for updates and creates).

ai_explanation.title: Ai Explanation
ai_explanation.description: AI-generated explanation of why this change was proposed.

PendingChange.title: PendingChange
PendingChange.description: A proposed document change awaiting user approval.

portal_url.title: Portal Url
portal_url.description: URL to the billing portal for managing subscription, invoices, and payment method.

session_id.title: Session Id
session_id.description: Session ID to load this document/attachment into.

parse_mode.title: Parse Mode
parse_mode.description: 'document' = parse and load as the active editable doc (returns parsed HTML); 'attachment' = process asynchronously as AI-searchable reference (returns job_id to poll).

RedeemRequest.title: RedeemRequest
RedeemRequest.description: Redeem a promo code and receive its credit grant.

RedeemResponse.title: RedeemResponse
RedeemResponse.description: Response body for POST /v1/promo/redeem on success.

RedemptionOut.title: RedemptionOut
RedemptionOut.description: Summary of a successful redemption, returned from POST /v1/promo/redeem.

user_id.title: User Id
user_id.description: User who owns this session (null for organization sessions).

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the session was created.

last_activity.title: Last Activity
last_activity.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent activity.

message_count.title: Message Count
message_count.description: Total number of messages in this session.

preview.title: Preview
preview.description: Preview of the first user message (up to 100 characters).

SessionInfo.title: SessionInfo
SessionInfo.description: Summary of a document editing session.

sessions.title: Sessions
sessions.description: Array of session summaries, ordered by most recent activity.

message.title: Message
message.description: Your message to the AI assistant.

session_id.title: Session Id
session_id.description: Unique session identifier. Reuse to continue a conversation.

document_html.title: Document Html
document_html.description: Current document HTML. Include data-chunk-id attributes on elements to enable targeted AI edits.

user_id.title: User Id
user_id.description: User identifier. Automatically set from authentication — typically omit this.

model_tier.title: Model Tier
model_tier.description: AI model to use: 'core' (default, fast), 'turbo' (fastest), 'pro' (advanced reasoning), 'max' (most capable).

thinking_depth.title: Thinking Depth
thinking_depth.description: Reasoning depth: 'fast', 'balanced' (default), or 'deep'. Controls how much analysis the AI performs.

approval_mode.title: Approval Mode
approval_mode.description: Change review mode: 'approve_all' (default, auto-applies changes) or 'ask_every_time' (pauses for your review).

response_mode.title: Response Mode
response_mode.description: Response shape control. 'full' (default) returns the complete updated document HTML — required by web app editors and recommended for small documents (<20 pages). 'compact' (recommended for AI agents editing large documents) suppresses the full HTML and returns only per-section diffs (chunk_diffs) for changed sections, saving thousands of tokens per turn. To read sections in compact mode, just send a natural-language request like 'show me the force majeure section' — the AI returns the content in the chat reply text.

UniversalChatRequest.title: UniversalChatRequest
UniversalChatRequest.description: Send a message to the AI assistant with optional document context.

examples.message: Add a confidentiality clause to section 3

filename.title: Filename
filename.description: Original filename including extension (e.g. 'contract.docx'). Used downstream for file-type detection.

content_type.title: Content Type
content_type.description: MIME type the agent will PUT with (e.g. 'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document' for .docx).

size_bytes.title: Size Bytes
size_bytes.description: File size in bytes. Must be > 0 and <= 104857600 (100 MB).

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.description: What this file will be used for. 'document' = the active editable doc; 'attachment' = read-only AI-searchable reference.

monthly_used.title: Monthly Used
monthly_used.description: Total operations used this billing cycle.

monthly_limit.title: Monthly Limit
monthly_limit.description: Maximum operations allowed per cycle. -1 means unlimited.

monthly_remaining.title: Monthly Remaining
monthly_remaining.description: Operations remaining this cycle. -1 means unlimited.

was_billable.title: Was Billable
was_billable.description: Whether this request counted as a billable operation.

bucket_used.title: Bucket Used
bucket_used.description: Which bucket this operation drew from: 'tier' or 'promo'. Null for non-billable ops.

redemption_id.title: Redemption Id
redemption_id.description: Redemption grant this op drew from, when bucket_used is 'promo'.

promotions.title: Promotions
promotions.description: User's currently-active promotion grants, ordered by oldest-expiring first.

UsageInfo.title: UsageInfo
UsageInfo.description: Operation usage data for the current billing cycle.

monthly_limit.title: Monthly Limit
monthly_limit.description: Maximum operations allowed per month. -1 means unlimited.

monthly_reset_at.title: Monthly Reset At
monthly_reset_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the monthly counter resets.

total_sessions.title: Total Sessions
total_sessions.description: Total number of chat sessions created.

total_operations.title: Total Operations
total_operations.description: Lifetime total operations across all billing cycles.

current_month_stats.title: Current Month Stats
current_month_stats.description: Breakdown of this month's operations by type, including counts, tokens used, and success rates.

UsageStatsResponse.title: UsageStatsResponse
UsageStatsResponse.description: Detailed usage statistics for the current billing cycle.

email_verified.title: Email Verified
email_verified.description: Whether the user's email has been verified.

monthly_operation_limit.title: Monthly Operation Limit
monthly_operation_limit.description: Maximum operations allowed per month. -1 means unlimited.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the account was created.

last_login_at.title: Last Login At
last_login_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the last login.

UserProfileResponse.title: UserProfileResponse
UserProfileResponse.description: User profile with subscription and usage information.

UserPromotionsResponse.title: UserPromotionsResponse
UserPromotionsResponse.description: Listing of the authenticated user's promotion grants, split by active vs. history.

last_message_at.title: Last Message At
last_message_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent message.

message_count.title: Message Count
message_count.description: Total number of messages in this session.

UserSessionResponse.title: UserSessionResponse
UserSessionResponse.description: Summary of a user's chat session.
